target_comparison.explanation: High ambiguity remains undesirable even when style looks promising.
Boards should feel coherent while still offering some trickiness.
Groups should expose explicit evidence and understandable rule provenance.
Category labels should stay concise and interpretable.
Label shape should feel consistent across the board without becoming uniform.
Phonetic groups should remain a precise showcase mechanism rather than a majority.
Avoid repeated mechanism families and repetitive label shapes.
Keep semantic groups central without letting them dominate every board.
Balance semantic categories against lexical and phonetic wordplay.
Boards should land within a majority of the explicit Stage 3 style bands.
Theme groups are allowed but should stay occasional.
Prefer mixed boards over single-mechanism monotony.
Top boards should usually include at least one lexical or phonetic wordplay group.

notes: Stage 3 calibration summary compares batch slices against explicit local target bands.
These comparisons are intended for offline review, not hidden optimization.
Batch summary is built from the Stage 3 mixed-generation path plus the Stage 1 quality-control core and Stage 3 style-analysis artifacts.
